value_chain_position: Thin-film cadmium-telluride solar module manufacturer — solar-manufacturing layer of the energy-transition value chain, selling domestically produced utility-scale modules in the US and internationally.
relative_comps: FSLR is the only scaled domestic thin-film solar manufacturer, giving it a tariff moat that Chinese competitors (CSIQ, JKS) cannot easily replicate. FCF margin of 31% and net cash balance sheet are exceptional among solar peers — ENPH and SEDG compete on the inverter/software layer rather than module manufacturing. The 26% 3-year CAGR and 27% YoY growth reflect IRA manufacturing credits augmenting operational scale. Key risk is module commoditization and IRA policy dependency, which limits the load-bearing score to 3 despite the manufacturing moat.
